x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.101, p.044614 (2020)

x4freetext: The
experimental method and the analysis.

x4codes.hlp: Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America
Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America

x4freetext: Weapon Neutron Research (WNR)
facility of the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center
(LANSCE).
The LANSCE linear accelerator provided a
pulsed beam of 800 MeV protons impinging on a
tungsten target at WNR. Spallation reactions were
generated and produced a white neutron source, with
neutron energies ranging from below 1 MeV to about
700 MeV.
The setup was formed of a fission
chamber containing 22 235U samples, surrounded by the
54 liquid scintillators of the Chi-Nu array, placed 1
m away from the target position. The chamber was
filled with high-purity CF4 gas and operated at a
fixed pressure of about 900 mbar, 100 mbar above the
local atmospheric pressure. It allowed for the use of
thin structural material, thereby minimizing the
parasitic scattering of incoming and fission neutrons.
22 samples of 2.5 mg of uranium 235 were each
deposited on an aluminum backing within a 33 mm
diameter circle at the European Commission Joint
Research Center of Geel, Belgium. The 22 samples had
an isotopic purity above 99.9% and amount to a total
of 55 mg of radioactive material used as target for
the incident neutrons. The multiplate fission chamber
contained the stack of 22 samples, with their backing
serving as cathodes, and 11 anodes, assembled on
three insulating rods.
The double time of flight (ToF) method was used
to measure the incoming and prompt fission neutrons
energies. The detection of fission fragments inside
the fission chamber provided a reference time for
fission events. It was compared to the time of the
proton pulse, T0, which provided an effective neutron
time resolution of 0.5 ns or less, to determine the
ToF of the incoming neutron. Incoming neutron
energies were extracted event by event from the
length of the flight path and the measured ToF.
Following the fission event, the prompt fission
neutrons were detected in the liquid scintillators
forming the Chi-Nu array. Prompt fission neutron
energies were extracted from the ToF between the
fission chamber and the Chi-Nu detectors. The
experimental setup was placed on the 15 degree left
flight path (4FP15L), at 21.5 m from the production
target.
The so-called wraparound correction was needed
because of the pulsed structure of the beam. Beam
leakage current correction was needed because of an
issue with the LANSCE proton accelerator. Additional
corrections to the mean energy: to calculate the mean
energy over the full range of the actual PFNS, the
measured PFNS had to be extrapolated over the low
energy range. The simulated measurement of 235U(n,f)
spectra at each incoming neutron energy were
corrected for the intrinsic efficiency calculated
using the 252Cf(sf) simulated measurement, and
extrapolated to lower energies.
Approved by B.Mauss on August 29, 2025.
BP
